scope: M30 User account email edit unblock and domain-change audit
clientIssue: Client reported that user emails were changed to @Securecreditlines.com and could not be edited from Settings -> Users.

documentReview.finding: The document asks to update public website/legal page domain references and public contact email info@securecreditlines.com. It does not ask to change platform user login emails.
documentReview.policyApplied: Do not rewrite account emails unless the client explicitly provides the correct email for each user.

rootCause: Users edit modal rendered the Email input as disabled during edit mode.
Frontend edit payload did not include email.
Backend updateUser validation allowed email, but AuthController.updateUser ignored it.
Production had a dirty defaultUsers bootstrap change that could sync existing users back to default @securecreditlines.com emails on restart.
implementation: Email input is editable in Settings -> Users edit modal.
Edit payload now sends email to PUT /api/auth/users/:id.
AuthController.updateUser now persists email for ADMIN updates, rejects duplicate emails, and invalidates the user cache after update.
Default team bootstrap preserves existing user emails; production dirty email-sync code must not be reintroduced.

validation.vscodeDiagnostics: No errors found in UsersTab.tsx, authController.ts, and defaultUsers.ts.
validation.focusedTests: server npm test -- --run tests/authOtpPolicy.test.ts tests/authDevModeLogin.test.ts: 2 files passed, 8 tests passed.
validation.build: npm run build passed: server tsc and client tsc/vite build.
validation.productionUsersInspected: Production user emails were listed for diagnosis only; no bulk email data migration was performed.

remainingClientAction: Client/admin can now edit each user's email to the correct address in Settings -> Users after production deploy.
